response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of full-stack experience with direct TypeScript, Node.js, React, and MongoDB exposure — all core to this role. His most recent position at re:cinq is a near-perfect match: full-stack work, AI-agent infrastructure, and MCP tooling are explicitly listed and rare to find combined. A current caregiving gap (Oct 2025–present, ~7 months) is the main practical concern. Nest.js and Vitest are not explicitly mentioned, but his depth in the broader ecosystem and his history of picking up new frameworks quickly (Swift, DLang, Kotlin, Java) makes those gaps low-risk. His TDD advocacy and homegrown testing frameworks demonstrate a testing culture that aligns well with the role's quality expectations.
strengths: Direct AI-agent and MCP (Model Context Protocol) development experience from re:cinq — a rare and high-value match for the role's AI agent requirement
15+ years of full-stack depth across TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK, Docker, and Kubernetes — covers virtually the entire required stack
Strong TDD and software craftsmanship culture evidenced by authored testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), workshops, and explicit TDD/pairing/CI practices at RIDE Capital
concerns: Nest.js is not mentioned anywhere on the resume — it is the primary backend framework for this role and a non-trivial gap
Currently on a caregiving career gap (Oct 2025–present), meaning no active hands-on engineering for ~7 months at time of review
No explicit mention of Vitest or Playwright; testing framework experience is largely D-language specific, leaving uncertainty about Jest/Vitest familiarity in the TypeScript ecosystem
